text.title: Agent Limitations in Backlog Grooming
text.summary: Failure modes specific to LLM agents performing backlog grooming and roadmapping tasks.

antipattern.description: Agent-generated backlog items look certain and well-reasoned but are pure hypothesis.
antipattern.reason: LLMs produce polished text that creates false confidence. Text quality does not equal idea quality. Always mark AI-generated items as hypothesis and require user validation before writing a spec.

antipattern.description: Agent assigns 100% Confidence in RICE scores to almost all items.
antipattern.reason: Agents lack data to justify lower confidence, so they default to 100%. Require explicit justification for Confidence above 80% in agent-generated scores.

antipattern.description: Agent assigns high RICE scores to technically interesting items regardless of business value.
antipattern.reason: Agents cannot distinguish strategic P0s from tactical P0s without explicit business context. Human must validate strategic feasibility before items move to todo/.

antipattern.description: Agent adds items to backlog but never drops or archives old ones.
antipattern.reason: Agents reword and reorder but rarely delete. Backlogs grow unbounded without explicit cleanup instructions. Archive items older than 6 months with no progress to `.aidocs/backlog/.archive/`.

rule.statement: Explicitly instruct the agent to identify items for archival or deletion in every grooming session. Never let the agent add items without also reviewing items for removal.
rule.rationale: Passive accumulation defeats the purpose of grooming. A backlog that only grows degrades the signal-to-noise ratio for future prioritization.
